title: Streams, Redirection, Quoting, and Pipelines · Linux Stakeholder Mission
summary: A support lead needs a repeatable command notebook that separates report data from diagnostics. Deliver a pipeline that preserves quoting, descriptor routing, stage failure, and changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Incident Evidence Pipeline with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
Distinguish tilde, parameter, command, arithmetic, pathname, and quote-removal expansion before command execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Pipeline Status: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A 6-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build pipelines while accounting for per-stage exit status, pipefail, buffering, and stderr routing.
